{{ill|Shimazu Tadatsune|WD=Q1185372}}|father={{ill|Shimazu Takahisa|WD=Q2367308}}|relatives={{ill|Shimazu Iehisa|WD=Q1133219}}; {{ill|Shimazu Toshihisa|WD=Q860025}}; {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihisa|WD=Q955727}}|native_name=島津義弘|given_name={{ill|Yoshihiro|WD=Q8056051}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1156288]] Wikidata name: Shimazu Yoshihiro [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Samurai of the Sengoku period}} {{More citations needed|date=September 2022}} {{family name hatnote|Shimazu|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox officeholder | honorific_prefix = [[Senior Third Rank]] | name = Shimazu Yoshihiro | native_name = {{nobold|島津 義弘}} | native_name_lang = ja | commands = [[:en:Iino Castle|Iino Castle]] | predecessor = [[:en:Shimazu Yoshihisa|Shimazu Yoshihisa]] | termend = 1602 | termstart = 1587 | office = Head of [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]] | mother = Sesshō | father = [[:en:Shimazu Takahisa|Shimazu Takahisa]] | nationality = [[:en:Japanese people|Japanese]] | battles = {{tree list}} *'''Early Battles''' **[[:en:Battle of Kizaki|Battle of Kizaki]] **[[:en:Siege of Takabaru|Siege of Takabaru]] **[[:en:Battle of Mimigawa|Battle of Mimigawa]] **[[:en:Siege of Iwaya Castle|Siege of Iwaya Castle]] *'''[[:en:Kyūshū Campaign|Kyūshū Campaign]]''' **[[:en:siege of Kagoshima|siege of Kagoshima]] *'''[[:en:Japanese invasions of Korea (1592–1598)|Korean campaign]]''' **'''Imjin War''' ***[[:en:Gangwon campaign|Gangwon campaign]] **'''Jeongyu War''' ***Battle of Chilchonryang ***[[:en:Battle of Sacheon (1598)|Battle of Sacheon]] ***[[:en:Battle of Noryang|Battle of Noryang]] *'''[[:en:Sekigahara Campaign|Sekigahara Campaign]]''' **Battle of Sone castle **[[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] {{tree list/end}} | unit = [[File:Maru_juji.svg|15px]] [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]] | relations = [[:en:Shimazu Yoshihisa|Shimazu Yoshihisa]] (brother)
[[:en:Shimazu Toshihisa|Shimazu Toshihisa]] (brother)
[[:en:Shimazu Iehisa|Shimazu Iehisa]] (half-brother) | allegiance = [[File:Maru_juji.svg|15px]] [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]]
[[File:Goshichi no kiri inverted.svg|15px]] [[:en:Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi clan]]
[[File:大一大万大吉.svg|15px]] [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Western Army]] | nickname = "Shimazu Demon"
(Oni-Shimazu) | birth_place = [[:en:Izaku Castle|Izaku Castle]], [[:en:Satsuma Province|Satsuma Province]] | death_date = August 30, 1619 (aged 84) | birth_date = August 21, 1535 | caption = Shimazu Yoshihiro | image = Shimazu Yoshihiro.jpg | Rank = [[Daimyo]] | successor = [[:en:Shimazu Tadatsune|Shimazu Tadatsune]] | spouse = Hongō Tadataka's daughter
Saishō-dono | children = Shimazu Hisayasu
[[:en:Shimazu Tadatsune|Shimazu Tadatsune]] }} {{nihongo|'''Shimazu Yoshihiro'''|島津 義弘||August 21, 1535 – August 30, 1619}} was the second son of [[:en:Shimazu Takahisa|Shimazu Takahisa]] and the younger brother of [[:en:Shimazu Yoshihisa|Shimazu Yoshihisa]]. Traditionally believed to be the 17th head of the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]], he was a skilled general during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] who greatly contributed to the unification of [[Kyushu|Kyūshū]]. == Early life and rise == He is said to have been born in [[:en:Izaku Castle|Izaku Castle]] in 1535.{{cite web|url=http://www.m-satsuma.info/detail/tableware/pg167.html|title=国史跡 伊作城|publisher=南さつま観光|language=Japanese|access-date=25 July 2019|archive-date=5 August 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190805211029/http://www.m-satsuma.info/detail/tableware/pg167.html|url-status=dead}} He was the castle lord in command of [[:en:Iino Castle|Iino Castle]].{{cite web|url=https://kojodan.jp/castle/595/ |title=国史跡 飯野城|publisher=攻城団|language=Japanese|access-date=25 July 2019}} Yoshihiro along with [[:en:Niiro Tadamoto|Niiro Tadamoto]] defeated the [[:en:Itō clan|Itō clan]] at the [[:en:Battle of Kizaki|Battle of Kizaki]], in 1572, and defeated the [[:en:Otomo clan|Otomo clan]] with his brothers in the 1578 at [[:en:Battle of Mimigawa|Battle of Mimigawa]].{{cite book |last1=Turnbull |first1=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |date=2000 |publisher=Cassell & C0 |isbn=1-85409-523-4 |location=London |page=221,272–275}} In 1587, facing [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s troops that sought to pacify [[:en:Kyūshū Campaign|Kyūshū]], Yoshihiro pressed for war even after his brother and the head of clan Yoshihisa surrendered. After Yoshihisa repeatedly asked for the surrender, Yoshihiro finally accepted. After Yoshihisa became a Buddhist monk, it was believed that he became the head of the clan, though the real power remained in Yoshihisa's hands. ==Service under Hideyoshi== He had been a willing and skillful general under Toyotomi Hideyoshi. In both 1592 and 1597 of the Seven-Year War, Yoshihiro set his foot on the Korean peninsula and successfully carried out a series of battles. In 1597, working together with [[:en:Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takatora]], [[:en:Katō Yoshiaki|Katō Yoshiaki]] and [[:en:Konishi Yukinaga|Konishi Yukinaga]], Yoshihiro defeated [[:en:Won Kyun|Won Kyun]]'s navy. At the [[:en:Battle of Sacheon (1598)|Battle of Sacheon]] (泗川) in 1598, facing a Ming army counting 37,000, Yoshihiro defeated them with only 7,000 soldiers. On the final battle of the war in 1598, the [[:en:Battle of Noryang|Battle of Noryang]], Yoshihiro's objective was to cross the Noryang Strait, link up with Konishi and retreat to Japan.The Korean admiral [[:en:Yi Sun-sin|Yi Sun-sin]] who had obstructed Yoshiaki died in this battle. Afterward, Yoshihiro rescued the Japanese commanders and returned to Japan. == Battle of Sekigahara == [[File:Shimazu Yoshihiro Large Battle Standards.jpg|left|250px|thumb|Shimazu Yoshihiro Battle Standards]] After Toyotomi Hideyoshi's death in 1598, the land became split between Toyotomi supporters backing [[Ishida Mitsunari]] and those that supported [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]], eventually culminating in the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in 1600. According to novels ([[Rakusuishū]] 『落穂集』) of the Edo Period, Yoshihiro was initially supposed to Ieyasu's side. He was crushed by [[Ishida Mitsunari]]'s army upon his arrival for a rescue [[:en:Torii Mototada|Torii Mototada]] during the siege of Fushimi Castle and, after being humiliated, he took the side of Ishida Mitsunari instead. However, recent research indicated that Yoshihiro had chosen to side with Mitsunari from the very start, before [[:en:Mōri Terumoto|Mōri Terumoto]] announced the declaration of war against the Tokugawa in July 1600. Yoshihiro also played an important role as a member of Mitsunari's core staff and was pivotal in persuading [[:en:Uesugi Kagekatsu|Uesugi Kagekatsu]] to ally with them. On September 13, Shimazu Yoshihiro led his soldiers to besiege Sone Castle and bombarding Sone castle with their artilleries. [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] and [[:en:Honda Tadakatsu|Honda Tadakatsu]] implored [[:en:Mizuno Katsushige|Mizuno Katsunari]] to repulse the Shimazu forces. In response, Katsunari went out with sallying force with his younger brother, Mizuno Tadatane, to defend Sone Castle.{{sfn|Hirai|1992}} Katsunari ordered his artilleries to return fire at the turret of the Shimazu artilleries, then he led his army to storm the Shimazu position and manage to overcome the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]]'s army, causing Yoshihiro to retreat and abandon the siege against Sone castle.{{cite book |author1=Fujii Jizaemon |title=関ヶ原合戦史料集 |trans-title=Sekigahara Team History Collection |date=1979 |page=421|publisher=藤井治左衛門 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=WKXTAAAAMAAJ |access-date=23 May 2024 |language=Ja}}{{cite book |author1=Tadachika Kuwata |title=戦国時代の謎と怪異 |date=1977 |publisher=日本文芸社 |page=191 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=opvTAAAAMAAJ |access-date=23 May 2024 |language=Ja}}{{cite book |author1=Takashi Suzuki |title=大垣藩戶田家の見聞書 二百年間集積史料「御家耳袋」 |date=2006 |publisher=愛文書林 |isbn=4872940520 |page=32 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wBrjAAAAMAAJ |access-date=23 May 2024 |language=Ja}} After he beat the Shimazu forces, returned to Ieyasu to ask permission to participate on the Sekigahara main battle which planned in the next day. However, Ieyasu instead ordered Katsunari to guard Sone castle and keep an eyes on Ogaki castle which located nearby and being controlled by Western army loyal to Mitsunari. In response, Katsunari then brought his troops at midnight to surround Ogaki castle, beating a Western army led by Fukuhara Nagataka, and burning the wall and outer citadels of the castle.{{cite book |author1=岐阜県 |title=岐阜県史 Volume 6 |date=1965 |publisher=巌南堂書店 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pL9GAAAAMAAJ |access-date=21 May 2024 |language=Ja}}{{sfn|Hirai|1992}} As Katsunari burned the outer citadels of Ogaki castle,{{sfn|Hirai|1992}} the Western army nearby that area, including the Shimazu clan who had just been beaten by Katsunari's army, decided to abort their plan to go in as they thought the Ogaki castle could not be saved. Then Yoshihiro decided to retreat into [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]].{{efn|the memorandum about Sekigahara campaign has theorized that the castle was still not fallen at that moment. However, Yoshihiro saw the smoke soared high from the Ogaki castle and though the castle already fallen.{{cite book |title=大重平六覚書 |trans-title=Memorandum of Ōshige Heiroku |url=https://dc.lib.kagoshima-u.ac.jp/en/search/display/0102700001 |access-date=21 May 2024 |language=Ja}}}} According to the scripts of Yoshihiro's subordinate [[Kando Kutarō]], Yoshihiro got along with Mitsunari from the start, but their relationship was distorted by the novelists of the Edo Period. It was said that Mitsunari did not listen to any of Yoshihiro's plans, including a surprise night attack on the day before the actual battle of Sekigahara. On the day of the battle, Yoshihiro and his 1500 men held ground and did not fight at all. After the rest of Mitsunari's side was wiped out, Yoshihiro was stranded among at least 30,000 of Ieyasu's troops. Vastly outnumbered, Yoshihiro tried to make a charge against Ieyasu himself but after his nephew [[:en:Shimazu Toyohisa|Shimazu Toyohisa]] demanded that he not kill himself over a meaningless battle, Yoshihiro instead chose to charge straight through Ieyasu's troop to make an exit to the other side. Yoshihiro had his troops make a fighting retreat called ''Sutegamari'' (捨て奸), where until a certain number of men died holding a position and repelling an attack, the main body of the army fought as well. Though Toyohisa and the bulk of the troops died, the charge and retreat were a success with a wound dealt to [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] in the process. After beating back the chase, he picked up his wife at Sumiyoshi in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]] and returned to [[:en:Satsuma Province|Satsuma Province]] by ships. The Japanese historian [[Shiramine Jun]] studied why the Shimazu clan behaved torpidly and deduced it was because Yoshihiro had become involved in the power struggling between his brother Yoshihisa and [[Ijūin Tadamune]]. It led Yoshihiro losing the support of Yoshihisa, which trapped Yoshihiro due to the lack of support from the Shimazu clan. == Death == [[File:伊集院駅.jpg|right|280px|thumb|Statue of Yoshihiro at [[:en:Ijūin Station|Ijūin Station]]]] In 1602, after recognizing why and how Yoshihiro behaved on the battlefield, Ieyasu had the Shimazu clan retain its domain and let Yoshihiro's son [[:en:Shimazu Tadatsune|Shimazu Tadatsune]] succeed him. Yoshihiro retired to [[:en:Sakurajima|Sakurajima]] and took up teaching younger generations. He died in 1619 and several of his retainers who had fought alongside him followed him by committing [[:en:Suicide|Suicide]]. == Family == It had traditionally been believed that he became the seventeenth head of the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]] after Yoshihisa, but it is currently believed that he let Yoshihisa keep his position. Yoshihiro was essential to the Shimazu clan and both Ieyasu and Hideyoshi tried to divide the clan by treating Yoshihiro well, but treating the elder brother Yoshihisa badly, which did not succeed. He was a devoted [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], and built a monument for enemy troops during the [[:en:Japanese invasions of Korea (1592–1598)|Seven-Year War]]. ==In popular culture== ''See {{section link|People of the Sengoku period in popular culture|Shimazu Yoshihiro}}'' He is a playable character in ''[[:en:Pokémon Conquest|Pokémon Conquest]]'' (Pokémon + Nobunaga's Ambition in Japan), with his partner [[:en:Pokémon|Pokémon]] being [[:en:Gurdurr|Gurdurr]] and [[:en:Conkeldurr|Conkeldurr]].{{cite web |url=http://www.pokemonconquest.com/en-us/character/yoshihiro |title=Yoshihiro + Gurdurr - Pokemon Conquest characters|work=Pokemon|access-date=2012-06-17}} He is also a playable character in ''[[:en:Total War: Shogun 2|Total War: Shogun 2]]''. He is a playable character in the ''[[:en:Samurai Warriors|Samurai Warriors]]'' series of video games. He is a playable character in the ''[[:en:Sengoku Basara|Sengoku Basara]]'' series of video games. He is the main antagonist in [[:en:Noryang: Deadly Sea|Noryang: Deadly Sea]] and is portrayed by [[:en:Baek Yoon-sik|Baek Yoon-sik]]. == Appendix == === Footnotes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Shimazu Yoshihiro : 徳永真一郎 『島津義弘』（[[:en:光文社|光文社]]光文社文庫・1992） {{ISBN|4-334-71629-6}} later （学陽書房 人物文庫 2010th year ）{{ISBN|978-4-313-75264-1}} *Shimazu Yoshihiro : 加野厚志 『島津義弘』（PHP研究所/PHP文庫 1996） {{ISBN|4-569-56965-X}} *Shimazu Hashiru : 池宮彰一郎 『島津奔る』上下巻（[[:en:新潮社|新潮社]] 2001） Vol1. {{ISBN|4-10-140816-5}}, Vol2 {{ISBN|4-10-140817-3}} *Shimazu Yoshihiro : 江宮隆之 『島津義弘』（学研M文庫、2004 ）{{ISBN|4-05-901162-2}} *Sengokuishin Shimazutoeden : 荒川佳夫 『戦国維新 島津東征伝』全3巻（学研歴史群像新書、2004〜2006） Vol.1 {{ISBN|4-05-402676-1}}, Vol.2 {{ISBN|4-05-402864-0}} Vol.3 {{ISBN|4-05-403057-2}} *[http://www.shimazu-yoshihiro.com/ Shimazu-Yoshihiro.com -About Shimazu Yoshihiro] (Japanese) {{People of the Sengoku period|state=autocollapse}} {{Authority control}}]] {{draft categories| [[Category:1535 births]] [[Category:1619 deaths]] [[Category:Daimyo]] [[Category:People from Satsuma Domain]] [[Category:Shimazu clan]] [[Category:Deified Japanese men]] [[Category:People from Kagoshima Prefecture]] }} [[en:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ca:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[de:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[es:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[fa:شیمازو یوشی‌هیرو]] [[fi:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[fr:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[id:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[it:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[ja:島津義弘]] [[ko:시마즈 요시히로]] [[mr:शिमाझु योशिहिरो]] [[nl:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[pt:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[ru:Симадзу Ёсихиро]] [[th:ชิมาซุ โยชิฮิโระ]] [[tr:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[uk:Сімадзу Йосіхіро]] [[vi:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[zh:島津義弘]] [[zh-min-nan:Simazu Yosihiro]] {{wikidata link|Q1156288}}

(Oni-Shimazu) | birth_place = [[:en:Izaku Castle|Izaku Castle]], [[:en:Satsuma Province|Satsuma Province]] | death_date = August 30, 1619 (aged 84) | birth_date = August 21, 1535 | caption = Shimazu Yoshihiro | image = Shimazu Yoshihiro.jpg | Rank = [[Daimyo]] | successor = [[:en:Shimazu Tadatsune|Shimazu Tadatsune]] | spouse = Hongō Tadataka's daughter
Saishō-dono | children = Shimazu Hisayasu
[[:en:Shimazu Tadatsune|Shimazu Tadatsune]] }} {{nihongo|'''Shimazu Yoshihiro'''|島津 義弘||August 21, 1535 – August 30, 1619}} was the second son of [[:en:Shimazu Takahisa|Shimazu Takahisa]] and the younger brother of [[:en:Shimazu Yoshihisa|Shimazu Yoshihisa]]. Traditionally believed to be the 17th head of the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]], he was a skilled general during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] who greatly contributed to the unification of [[Kyushu|Kyūshū]]. == Early life and rise == He is said to have been born in [[:en:Izaku Castle|Izaku Castle]] in 1535.{{cite web|url=http://www.m-satsuma.info/detail/tableware/pg167.html|title=国史跡 伊作城|publisher=南さつま観光|language=Japanese|access-date=25 July 2019|archive-date=5 August 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190805211029/http://www.m-satsuma.info/detail/tableware/pg167.html|url-status=dead}} He was the castle lord in command of [[:en:Iino Castle|Iino Castle]].{{cite web|url=https://kojodan.jp/castle/595/ |title=国史跡 飯野城|publisher=攻城団|language=Japanese|access-date=25 July 2019}} Yoshihiro along with [[:en:Niiro Tadamoto|Niiro Tadamoto]] defeated the [[:en:Itō clan|Itō clan]] at the [[:en:Battle of Kizaki|Battle of Kizaki]], in 1572, and defeated the [[:en:Otomo clan|Otomo clan]] with his brothers in the 1578 at [[:en:Battle of Mimigawa|Battle of Mimigawa]].{{cite book |last1=Turnbull |first1=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |date=2000 |publisher=Cassell & C0 |isbn=1-85409-523-4 |location=London |page=221,272–275}} In 1587, facing [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s troops that sought to pacify [[:en:Kyūshū Campaign|Kyūshū]], Yoshihiro pressed for war even after his brother and the head of clan Yoshihisa surrendered. After Yoshihisa repeatedly asked for the surrender, Yoshihiro finally accepted. After Yoshihisa became a Buddhist monk, it was believed that he became the head of the clan, though the real power remained in Yoshihisa's hands. ==Service under Hideyoshi== He had been a willing and skillful general under Toyotomi Hideyoshi. In both 1592 and 1597 of the Seven-Year War, Yoshihiro set his foot on the Korean peninsula and successfully carried out a series of battles. In 1597, working together with [[:en:Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takatora]], [[:en:Katō Yoshiaki|Katō Yoshiaki]] and [[:en:Konishi Yukinaga|Konishi Yukinaga]], Yoshihiro defeated [[:en:Won Kyun|Won Kyun]]'s navy. At the [[:en:Battle of Sacheon (1598)|Battle of Sacheon]] (泗川) in 1598, facing a Ming army counting 37,000, Yoshihiro defeated them with only 7,000 soldiers. On the final battle of the war in 1598, the [[:en:Battle of Noryang|Battle of Noryang]], Yoshihiro's objective was to cross the Noryang Strait, link up with Konishi and retreat to Japan.The Korean admiral [[:en:Yi Sun-sin|Yi Sun-sin]] who had obstructed Yoshiaki died in this battle. Afterward, Yoshihiro rescued the Japanese commanders and returned to Japan. == Battle of Sekigahara == [[File:Shimazu Yoshihiro Large Battle Standards.jpg|left|250px|thumb|Shimazu Yoshihiro Battle Standards]] After Toyotomi Hideyoshi's death in 1598, the land became split between Toyotomi supporters backing [[Ishida Mitsunari]] and those that supported [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]], eventually culminating in the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in 1600. According to novels ([[Rakusuishū]] 『落穂集』) of the Edo Period, Yoshihiro was initially supposed to Ieyasu's side. He was crushed by [[Ishida Mitsunari]]'s army upon his arrival for a rescue [[:en:Torii Mototada|Torii Mototada]] during the siege of Fushimi Castle and, after being humiliated, he took the side of Ishida Mitsunari instead. However, recent research indicated that Yoshihiro had chosen to side with Mitsunari from the very start, before [[:en:Mōri Terumoto|Mōri Terumoto]] announced the declaration of war against the Tokugawa in July 1600. Yoshihiro also played an important role as a member of Mitsunari's core staff and was pivotal in persuading [[:en:Uesugi Kagekatsu|Uesugi Kagekatsu]] to ally with them. On September 13, Shimazu Yoshihiro led his soldiers to besiege Sone Castle and bombarding Sone castle with their artilleries. [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] and [[:en:Honda Tadakatsu|Honda Tadakatsu]] implored [[:en:Mizuno Katsushige|Mizuno Katsunari]] to repulse the Shimazu forces. In response, Katsunari went out with sallying force with his younger brother, Mizuno Tadatane, to defend Sone Castle.{{sfn|Hirai|1992}} Katsunari ordered his artilleries to return fire at the turret of the Shimazu artilleries, then he led his army to storm the Shimazu position and manage to overcome the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]]'s army, causing Yoshihiro to retreat and abandon the siege against Sone castle.{{cite book |author1=Fujii Jizaemon |title=関ヶ原合戦史料集 |trans-title=Sekigahara Team History Collection |date=1979 |page=421|publisher=藤井治左衛門 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=WKXTAAAAMAAJ |access-date=23 May 2024 |language=Ja}}{{cite book |author1=Tadachika Kuwata |title=戦国時代の謎と怪異 |date=1977 |publisher=日本文芸社 |page=191 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=opvTAAAAMAAJ |access-date=23 May 2024 |language=Ja}}{{cite book |author1=Takashi Suzuki |title=大垣藩戶田家の見聞書 二百年間集積史料「御家耳袋」 |date=2006 |publisher=愛文書林 |isbn=4872940520 |page=32 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wBrjAAAAMAAJ |access-date=23 May 2024 |language=Ja}} After he beat the Shimazu forces, returned to Ieyasu to ask permission to participate on the Sekigahara main battle which planned in the next day. However, Ieyasu instead ordered Katsunari to guard Sone castle and keep an eyes on Ogaki castle which located nearby and being controlled by Western army loyal to Mitsunari. In response, Katsunari then brought his troops at midnight to surround Ogaki castle, beating a Western army led by Fukuhara Nagataka, and burning the wall and outer citadels of the castle.{{cite book |author1=岐阜県 |title=岐阜県史 Volume 6 |date=1965 |publisher=巌南堂書店 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pL9GAAAAMAAJ |access-date=21 May 2024 |language=Ja}}{{sfn|Hirai|1992}} As Katsunari burned the outer citadels of Ogaki castle,{{sfn|Hirai|1992}} the Western army nearby that area, including the Shimazu clan who had just been beaten by Katsunari's army, decided to abort their plan to go in as they thought the Ogaki castle could not be saved. Then Yoshihiro decided to retreat into [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]].{{efn|the memorandum about Sekigahara campaign has theorized that the castle was still not fallen at that moment. However, Yoshihiro saw the smoke soared high from the Ogaki castle and though the castle already fallen.{{cite book |title=大重平六覚書 |trans-title=Memorandum of Ōshige Heiroku |url=https://dc.lib.kagoshima-u.ac.jp/en/search/display/0102700001 |access-date=21 May 2024 |language=Ja}}}} According to the scripts of Yoshihiro's subordinate [[Kando Kutarō]], Yoshihiro got along with Mitsunari from the start, but their relationship was distorted by the novelists of the Edo Period. It was said that Mitsunari did not listen to any of Yoshihiro's plans, including a surprise night attack on the day before the actual battle of Sekigahara. On the day of the battle, Yoshihiro and his 1500 men held ground and did not fight at all. After the rest of Mitsunari's side was wiped out, Yoshihiro was stranded among at least 30,000 of Ieyasu's troops. Vastly outnumbered, Yoshihiro tried to make a charge against Ieyasu himself but after his nephew [[:en:Shimazu Toyohisa|Shimazu Toyohisa]] demanded that he not kill himself over a meaningless battle, Yoshihiro instead chose to charge straight through Ieyasu's troop to make an exit to the other side. Yoshihiro had his troops make a fighting retreat called ''Sutegamari'' (捨て奸), where until a certain number of men died holding a position and repelling an attack, the main body of the army fought as well. Though Toyohisa and the bulk of the troops died, the charge and retreat were a success with a wound dealt to [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] in the process. After beating back the chase, he picked up his wife at Sumiyoshi in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]] and returned to [[:en:Satsuma Province|Satsuma Province]] by ships. The Japanese historian [[Shiramine Jun]] studied why the Shimazu clan behaved torpidly and deduced it was because Yoshihiro had become involved in the power struggling between his brother Yoshihisa and [[Ijūin Tadamune]]. It led Yoshihiro losing the support of Yoshihisa, which trapped Yoshihiro due to the lack of support from the Shimazu clan. == Death == [[File:伊集院駅.jpg|right|280px|thumb|Statue of Yoshihiro at [[:en:Ijūin Station|Ijūin Station]]]] In 1602, after recognizing why and how Yoshihiro behaved on the battlefield, Ieyasu had the Shimazu clan retain its domain and let Yoshihiro's son [[:en:Shimazu Tadatsune|Shimazu Tadatsune]] succeed him. Yoshihiro retired to [[:en:Sakurajima|Sakurajima]] and took up teaching younger generations. He died in 1619 and several of his retainers who had fought alongside him followed him by committing [[:en:Suicide|Suicide]]. == Family == It had traditionally been believed that he became the seventeenth head of the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]] after Yoshihisa, but it is currently believed that he let Yoshihisa keep his position. Yoshihiro was essential to the Shimazu clan and both Ieyasu and Hideyoshi tried to divide the clan by treating Yoshihiro well, but treating the elder brother Yoshihisa badly, which did not succeed. He was a devoted [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], and built a monument for enemy troops during the [[:en:Japanese invasions of Korea (1592–1598)|Seven-Year War]]. ==In popular culture== ''See {{section link|People of the Sengoku period in popular culture|Shimazu Yoshihiro}}'' He is a playable character in ''[[:en:Pokémon Conquest|Pokémon Conquest]]'' (Pokémon + Nobunaga's Ambition in Japan), with his partner [[:en:Pokémon|Pokémon]] being [[:en:Gurdurr|Gurdurr]] and [[:en:Conkeldurr|Conkeldurr]].{{cite web |url=http://www.pokemonconquest.com/en-us/character/yoshihiro |title=Yoshihiro + Gurdurr - Pokemon Conquest characters|work=Pokemon|access-date=2012-06-17}} He is also a playable character in ''[[:en:Total War: Shogun 2|Total War: Shogun 2]]''. He is a playable character in the ''[[:en:Samurai Warriors|Samurai Warriors]]'' series of video games. He is a playable character in the ''[[:en:Sengoku Basara|Sengoku Basara]]'' series of video games. He is the main antagonist in [[:en:Noryang: Deadly Sea|Noryang: Deadly Sea]] and is portrayed by [[:en:Baek Yoon-sik|Baek Yoon-sik]]. == Appendix == === Footnotes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Shimazu Yoshihiro : 徳永真一郎 『島津義弘』（[[:en:光文社|光文社]]光文社文庫・1992） {{ISBN|4-334-71629-6}} later （学陽書房 人物文庫 2010th year ）{{ISBN|978-4-313-75264-1}} *Shimazu Yoshihiro : 加野厚志 『島津義弘』（PHP研究所/PHP文庫 1996） {{ISBN|4-569-56965-X}} *Shimazu Hashiru : 池宮彰一郎 『島津奔る』上下巻（[[:en:新潮社|新潮社]] 2001） Vol1. {{ISBN|4-10-140816-5}}, Vol2 {{ISBN|4-10-140817-3}} *Shimazu Yoshihiro : 江宮隆之 『島津義弘』（学研M文庫、2004 ）{{ISBN|4-05-901162-2}} *Sengokuishin Shimazutoeden : 荒川佳夫 『戦国維新 島津東征伝』全3巻（学研歴史群像新書、2004〜2006） Vol.1 {{ISBN|4-05-402676-1}}, Vol.2 {{ISBN|4-05-402864-0}} Vol.3 {{ISBN|4-05-403057-2}} *[http://www.shimazu-yoshihiro.com/ Shimazu-Yoshihiro.com -About Shimazu Yoshihiro] (Japanese) {{People of the Sengoku period|state=autocollapse}} {{Authority control}}]] {{draft categories| [[Category:1535 births]] [[Category:1619 deaths]] [[Category:Daimyo]] [[Category:People from Satsuma Domain]] [[Category:Shimazu clan]] [[Category:Deified Japanese men]] [[Category:People from Kagoshima Prefecture]] }} [[en:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ca:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[de:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[es:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[fa:شیمازو یوشی‌هیرو]] [[fi:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[fr:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[id:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[it:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[ja:島津義弘]] [[ko:시마즈 요시히로]] [[mr:शिमाझु योशिहिरो]] [[nl:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[pt:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[ru:Симадзу Ёсихиро]] [[th:ชิมาซุ โยชิฮิโระ]] [[tr:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[uk:Сімадзу Йосіхіро]] [[vi:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[zh:島津義弘]] [[zh-min-nan:Simazu Yosihiro]] {{wikidata link|Q1156288}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[:en:Shimazu Tadatsune|Shimazu Tadatsune]] }} {{nihongo|'''Shimazu Yoshihiro'''|島津 義弘||August 21, 1535 – August 30, 1619}} was the second son of [[:en:Shimazu Takahisa|Shimazu Takahisa]] and the younger brother of [[:en:Shimazu Yoshihisa|Shimazu Yoshihisa]]. After Yoshihisa became a Buddhist monk, it was believed that he became the head of the clan, though the real power remained in Yoshihisa's hands. ==Service under Hideyoshi== He had been a willing and skillful general under Toyotomi Hideyoshi. In both 1592 and 1597 of the Seven-Year War, Yoshihiro set his foot on the Korean peninsula and successfully carried out a series of battles. In 1597, working together with [[:en:Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takatora]], [[:en:Katō Yoshiaki|Katō Yoshiaki]] and [[:en:Konishi Yukinaga|Konishi Yukinaga]], Yoshihiro defeated [[:en:Won Kyun|Won Kyun]]'s navy. At the [[:en:Battle of Sacheon (1598)|Battle of Sacheon]] (泗川) in 1598, facing a Ming army counting 37,000, Yoshihiro defeated them with only 7,000 soldiers. On the final battle of the war in 1598, the [[:en:Battle of Noryang|Battle of Noryang]], Yoshihiro's objective was to cross the Noryang Strait, link up with Konishi and retreat to Japan.The Korean admiral [[:en:Yi Sun-sin|Yi Sun-sin]] who had obstructed Yoshiaki died in this battle. Afterward, Yoshihiro rescued the Japanese commanders and returned to Japan. == Battle of Sekigahara == [[File:Shimazu Yoshihiro Large Battle Standards.jpg|left|250px|thumb|Shimazu Yoshihiro Battle Standards]] After Toyotomi Hideyoshi's death in 1598, the land became split between Toyotomi supporters backing [[Ishida Mitsunari]] and those that supported [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]], eventually culminating in the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in 1600. According to novels ([[Rakusuishū]] 『落穂集』) of the Edo Period, Yoshihiro was initially supposed to Ieyasu's side. He was crushed by [[Ishida Mitsunari]]'s army upon his arrival for a rescue [[:en:Torii Mototada|Torii Mototada]] during the siege of Fushimi Castle and, after being humiliated, he took the side of Ishida Mitsunari instead. However, recent research indicated that Yoshihiro had chosen to side with Mitsunari from the very start, before [[:en:Mōri Terumoto|Mōri Terumoto]] announced the declaration of war against the Tokugawa in July 1600. Yoshihiro also played an important role as a member of Mitsunari's core staff and was pivotal in persuading [[:en:Uesugi Kagekatsu|Uesugi Kagekatsu]] to ally with them. On September 13, Shimazu Yoshihiro led his soldiers to besiege Sone Castle and bombarding Sone castle with their artilleries. [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] and [[:en:Honda Tadakatsu|Honda Tadakatsu]] implored [[:en:Mizuno Katsushige|Mizuno Katsunari]] to repulse the Shimazu forces. In response, Katsunari went out with sallying force with his younger brother, Mizuno Tadatane, to defend Sone Castle.{{sfn|Hirai|1992}} Katsunari ordered his artilleries to return fire at the turret of the Shimazu artilleries, then he led his army to storm the Shimazu position and manage to overcome the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]]'s army, causing Yoshihiro to retreat and abandon the siege against Sone castle.{{cite book |author1=Fujii Jizaemon |title=関ヶ原合戦史料集 |trans-title=Sekigahara Team History Collection |date=1979 |page=421|publisher=藤井治左衛門 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=WKXTAAAAMAAJ |access-date=23 May 2024 |language=Ja}}{{cite book |author1=Tadachika Kuwata |title=戦国時代の謎と怪異 |date=1977 |publisher=日本文芸社 |page=191 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=opvTAAAAMAAJ |access-date=23 May 2024 |language=Ja}}{{cite book |author1=Takashi Suzuki |title=大垣藩戶田家の見聞書 二百年間集積史料「御家耳袋」 |date=2006 |publisher=愛文書林 |isbn=4872940520 |page=32 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wBrjAAAAMAAJ |access-date=23 May 2024 |language=Ja}} After he beat the Shimazu forces, returned to Ieyasu to ask permission to participate on the Sekigahara main battle which planned in the next day. However, Ieyasu instead ordered Katsunari to guard Sone castle and keep an eyes on Ogaki castle which located nearby and being controlled by Western army loyal to Mitsunari. In response, Katsunari then brought his troops at midnight to surround Ogaki castle, beating a Western army led by Fukuhara Nagataka, and burning the wall and outer citadels of the castle.{{cite book |author1=岐阜県 |title=岐阜県史 Volume 6 |date=1965 |publisher=巌南堂書店 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pL9GAAAAMAAJ |access-date=21 May 2024 |language=Ja}}{{sfn|Hirai|1992}} As Katsunari burned the outer citadels of Ogaki castle,{{sfn|Hirai|1992}} the Western army nearby that area, including the Shimazu clan who had just been beaten by Katsunari's army, decided to abort their plan to go in as they thought the Ogaki castle could not be saved. Then Yoshihiro decided to retreat into [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]].{{efn|the memorandum about Sekigahara campaign has theorized that the castle was still not fallen at that moment. However, Yoshihiro saw the smoke soared high from the Ogaki castle and though the castle already fallen.{{cite book |title=大重平六覚書 |trans-title=Memorandum of Ōshige Heiroku |url=https://dc.lib.kagoshima-u.ac.jp/en/search/display/0102700001 |access-date=21 May 2024 |language=Ja}}}} According to the scripts of Yoshihiro's subordinate [[Kando Kutarō]], Yoshihiro got along with Mitsunari from the start, but their relationship was distorted by the novelists of the Edo Period. It was said that Mitsunari did not listen to any of Yoshihiro's plans, including a surprise night attack on the day before the actual battle of Sekigahara. On the day of the battle, Yoshihiro and his 1500 men held ground and did not fight at all. After the rest of Mitsunari's side was wiped out, Yoshihiro was stranded among at least 30,000 of Ieyasu's troops. Vastly outnumbered, Yoshihiro tried to make a charge against Ieyasu himself but after his nephew [[:en:Shimazu Toyohisa|Shimazu Toyohisa]] demanded that he not kill himself over a meaningless battle, Yoshihiro instead chose to charge straight through Ieyasu's troop to make an exit to the other side. Yoshihiro had his troops make a fighting retreat called ''Sutegamari'' (捨て奸), where until a certain number of men died holding a position and repelling an attack, the main body of the army fought as well. Though Toyohisa and the bulk of the troops died, the charge and retreat were a success with a wound dealt to [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] in the process. After beating back the chase, he picked up his wife at Sumiyoshi in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]] and returned to [[:en:Satsuma Province|Satsuma Province]] by ships. The Japanese historian [[Shiramine Jun]] studied why the Shimazu clan behaved torpidly and deduced it was because Yoshihiro had become involved in the power struggling between his brother Yoshihisa and [[Ijūin Tadamune]]. It led Yoshihiro losing the support of Yoshihisa, which trapped Yoshihiro due to the lack of support from the Shimazu clan. == Death == [[File:伊集院駅.jpg|right|280px|thumb|Statue of Yoshihiro at [[:en:Ijūin Station|Ijūin Station]]]] In 1602, after recognizing why and how Yoshihiro behaved on the battlefield, Ieyasu had the Shimazu clan retain its domain and let Yoshihiro's son [[:en:Shimazu Tadatsune|Shimazu Tadatsune]] succeed him. Yoshihiro retired to [[:en:Sakurajima|Sakurajima]] and took up teaching younger generations. He died in 1619 and several of his retainers who had fought alongside him followed him by committing [[:en:Suicide|Suicide]]. == Family == It had traditionally been believed that he became the seventeenth head of the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]] after Yoshihisa, but it is currently believed that he let Yoshihisa keep his position. Yoshihiro was essential to the Shimazu clan and both Ieyasu and Hideyoshi tried to divide the clan by treating Yoshihiro well, but treating the elder brother Yoshihisa badly, which did not succeed. He was a devoted [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], and built a monument for enemy troops during the [[:en:Japanese invasions of Korea (1592–1598)|Seven-Year War]]. ==In popular culture== ''See {{section link|People of the Sengoku period in popular culture|Shimazu Yoshihiro}}'' He is a playable character in ''[[:en:Pokémon Conquest|Pokémon Conquest]]'' (Pokémon + Nobunaga's Ambition in Japan), with his partner [[:en:Pokémon|Pokémon]] being [[:en:Gurdurr|Gurdurr]] and [[:en:Conkeldurr|Conkeldurr]].{{cite web |url=http://www.pokemonconquest.com/en-us/character/yoshihiro |title=Yoshihiro + Gurdurr - Pokemon Conquest characters|work=Pokemon|access-date=2012-06-17}} He is also a playable character in ''[[:en:Total War: Shogun 2|Total War: Shogun 2]]''. He is a playable character in the ''[[:en:Samurai Warriors|Samurai Warriors]]'' series of video games. He is a playable character in the ''[[:en:Sengoku Basara|Sengoku Basara]]'' series of video games. He is the main antagonist in [[:en:Noryang: Deadly Sea|Noryang: Deadly Sea]] and is portrayed by [[:en:Baek Yoon-sik|Baek Yoon-sik]]. == Appendix == === Footnotes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Shimazu Yoshihiro : 徳永真一郎 『島津義弘』（[[:en:光文社|光文社]]光文社文庫・1992） {{ISBN|4-334-71629-6}} later （学陽書房 人物文庫 2010th year ）{{ISBN|978-4-313-75264-1}} *Shimazu Yoshihiro : 加野厚志 『島津義弘』（PHP研究所/PHP文庫 1996） {{ISBN|4-569-56965-X}} *Shimazu Hashiru : 池宮彰一郎 『島津奔る』上下巻（[[:en:新潮社|新潮社]] 2001） Vol1. {{ISBN|4-10-140816-5}}, Vol2 {{ISBN|4-10-140817-3}} *Shimazu Yoshihiro : 江宮隆之 『島津義弘』（学研M文庫、2004 ）{{ISBN|4-05-901162-2}} *Sengokuishin Shimazutoeden : 荒川佳夫 『戦国維新 島津東征伝』全3巻（学研歴史群像新書、2004〜2006） Vol.1 {{ISBN|4-05-402676-1}}, Vol.2 {{ISBN|4-05-402864-0}} Vol.3 {{ISBN|4-05-403057-2}} *[http://www.shimazu-yoshihiro.com/ Shimazu-Yoshihiro.com -About Shimazu Yoshihiro] (Japanese) {{People of the Sengoku period|state=autocollapse}} {{Authority control}}]] {{draft categories| [[Category:1535 births]] [[Category:1619 deaths]] [[Category:Daimyo]] [[Category:People from Satsuma Domain]] [[Category:Shimazu clan]] [[Category:Deified Japanese men]] [[Category:People from Kagoshima Prefecture]] }} [[en:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ca:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[de:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[es:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[fa:شیمازو یوشی‌هیرو]] [[fi:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[fr:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[id:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[it:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[ja:島津義弘]] [[ko:시마즈 요시히로]] [[mr:शिमाझु योशिहिरो]] [[nl:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[pt:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[ru:Симадзу Ёсихиро]] [[th:ชิมาซุ โยชิฮิโระ]] [[tr:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[uk:Сімадзу Йосіхіро]] [[vi:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[zh:島津義弘]] [[zh-min-nan:Simazu Yosihiro]] {{wikidata link|Q1156288}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

{{ill|Shimazu Tadatsune|WD=Q1185372}}|father={{ill|Shimazu Takahisa|WD=Q2367308}}|relatives={{ill|Shimazu Iehisa|WD=Q1133219}}; {{ill|Shimazu Toshihisa|WD=Q860025}}; {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihisa|WD=Q955727}}|native_name=島津義弘|given_name={{ill|Yoshihiro|WD=Q8056051}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1156288]] Wikidata name: Shimazu Yoshihiro [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Samurai of the Sengoku period}} {{More citations needed|date=September 2022}} {{family name hatnote|Shimazu|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox officeholder | honorific_prefix = [[Senior Third Rank]] | name = Shimazu Yoshihiro | native_name = {{nobold|島津 義弘}} | native_name_lang = ja | commands = [[:en:Iino Castle|Iino Castle]] | predecessor = [[:en:Shimazu Yoshihisa|Shimazu Yoshihisa]] | termend = 1602 | termstart = 1587 | office = Head of [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]] | mother = Sesshō | father = [[:en:Shimazu Takahisa|Shimazu Takahisa]] | nationality = [[:en:Japanese people|Japanese]] | battles = {{tree list}} *'''Early Battles''' **[[:en:Battle of Kizaki|Battle of Kizaki]] **[[:en:Siege of Takabaru|Siege of Takabaru]] **[[:en:Battle of Mimigawa|Battle of Mimigawa]] **[[:en:Siege of Iwaya Castle|Siege of Iwaya Castle]] *'''[[:en:Kyūshū Campaign|Kyūshū Campaign]]''' **[[:en:siege of Kagoshima|siege of Kagoshima]] *'''[[:en:Japanese invasions of Korea (1592–1598)|Korean campaign]]''' **'''Imjin War''' ***[[:en:Gangwon campaign|Gangwon campaign]] **'''Jeongyu War''' ***Battle of Chilchonryang ***[[:en:Battle of Sacheon (1598)|Battle of Sacheon]] ***[[:en:Battle of Noryang|Battle of Noryang]] *'''[[:en:Sekigahara Campaign|Sekigahara Campaign]]''' **Battle of Sone castle **[[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] {{tree list/end}} | unit = [[File:Maru_juji.svg|15px]] [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]] | relations = [[:en:Shimazu Yoshihisa|Shimazu Yoshihisa]] (brother)

(Oni-Shimazu) | birth_place = [[:en:Izaku Castle|Izaku Castle]], [[:en:Satsuma Province|Satsuma Province]] | death_date = August 30, 1619 (aged 84) | birth_date = August 21, 1535 | caption = Shimazu Yoshihiro | image = Shimazu Yoshihiro.jpg | Rank = [[Daimyo]] | successor = [[:en:Shimazu Tadatsune|Shimazu Tadatsune]] | spouse = Hongō Tadataka's daughter
Saishō-dono | children = Shimazu Hisayasu
[[:en:Shimazu Tadatsune|Shimazu Tadatsune]] }} {{nihongo|'''Shimazu Yoshihiro'''|島津 義弘||August 21, 1535 – August 30, 1619}} was the second son of [[:en:Shimazu Takahisa|Shimazu Takahisa]] and the younger brother of [[:en:Shimazu Yoshihisa|Shimazu Yoshihisa]]. Traditionally believed to be the 17th head of the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]], he was a skilled general during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] who greatly contributed to the unification of [[Kyushu|Kyūshū]]. == Early life and rise == He is said to have been born in [[:en:Izaku Castle|Izaku Castle]] in 1535.{{cite web|url=http://www.m-satsuma.info/detail/tableware/pg167.html|title=国史跡 伊作城|publisher=南さつま観光|language=Japanese|access-date=25 July 2019|archive-date=5 August 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190805211029/http://www.m-satsuma.info/detail/tableware/pg167.html|url-status=dead}} He was the castle lord in command of [[:en:Iino Castle|Iino Castle]].{{cite web|url=https://kojodan.jp/castle/595/ |title=国史跡 飯野城|publisher=攻城団|language=Japanese|access-date=25 July 2019}} Yoshihiro along with [[:en:Niiro Tadamoto|Niiro Tadamoto]] defeated the [[:en:Itō clan|Itō clan]] at the [[:en:Battle of Kizaki|Battle of Kizaki]], in 1572, and defeated the [[:en:Otomo clan|Otomo clan]] with his brothers in the 1578 at [[:en:Battle of Mimigawa|Battle of Mimigawa]].{{cite book |last1=Turnbull |first1=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |date=2000 |publisher=Cassell & C0 |isbn=1-85409-523-4 |location=London |page=221,272–275}} In 1587, facing [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s troops that sought to pacify [[:en:Kyūshū Campaign|Kyūshū]], Yoshihiro pressed for war even after his brother and the head of clan Yoshihisa surrendered. After Yoshihisa repeatedly asked for the surrender, Yoshihiro finally accepted. After Yoshihisa became a Buddhist monk, it was believed that he became the head of the clan, though the real power remained in Yoshihisa's hands. ==Service under Hideyoshi== He had been a willing and skillful general under Toyotomi Hideyoshi. In both 1592 and 1597 of the Seven-Year War, Yoshihiro set his foot on the Korean peninsula and successfully carried out a series of battles. In 1597, working together with [[:en:Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takatora]], [[:en:Katō Yoshiaki|Katō Yoshiaki]] and [[:en:Konishi Yukinaga|Konishi Yukinaga]], Yoshihiro defeated [[:en:Won Kyun|Won Kyun]]'s navy. At the [[:en:Battle of Sacheon (1598)|Battle of Sacheon]] (泗川) in 1598, facing a Ming army counting 37,000, Yoshihiro defeated them with only 7,000 soldiers. On the final battle of the war in 1598, the [[:en:Battle of Noryang|Battle of Noryang]], Yoshihiro's objective was to cross the Noryang Strait, link up with Konishi and retreat to Japan.The Korean admiral [[:en:Yi Sun-sin|Yi Sun-sin]] who had obstructed Yoshiaki died in this battle. Afterward, Yoshihiro rescued the Japanese commanders and returned to Japan. == Battle of Sekigahara == [[File:Shimazu Yoshihiro Large Battle Standards.jpg|left|250px|thumb|Shimazu Yoshihiro Battle Standards]] After Toyotomi Hideyoshi's death in 1598, the land became split between Toyotomi supporters backing [[Ishida Mitsunari]] and those that supported [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]], eventually culminating in the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in 1600. According to novels ([[Rakusuishū]] 『落穂集』) of the Edo Period, Yoshihiro was initially supposed to Ieyasu's side. He was crushed by [[Ishida Mitsunari]]'s army upon his arrival for a rescue [[:en:Torii Mototada|Torii Mototada]] during the siege of Fushimi Castle and, after being humiliated, he took the side of Ishida Mitsunari instead. However, recent research indicated that Yoshihiro had chosen to side with Mitsunari from the very start, before [[:en:Mōri Terumoto|Mōri Terumoto]] announced the declaration of war against the Tokugawa in July 1600. Yoshihiro also played an important role as a member of Mitsunari's core staff and was pivotal in persuading [[:en:Uesugi Kagekatsu|Uesugi Kagekatsu]] to ally with them. On September 13, Shimazu Yoshihiro led his soldiers to besiege Sone Castle and bombarding Sone castle with their artilleries. [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] and [[:en:Honda Tadakatsu|Honda Tadakatsu]] implored [[:en:Mizuno Katsushige|Mizuno Katsunari]] to repulse the Shimazu forces. In response, Katsunari went out with sallying force with his younger brother, Mizuno Tadatane, to defend Sone Castle.{{sfn|Hirai|1992}} Katsunari ordered his artilleries to return fire at the turret of the Shimazu artilleries, then he led his army to storm the Shimazu position and manage to overcome the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]]'s army, causing Yoshihiro to retreat and abandon the siege against Sone castle.{{cite book |author1=Fujii Jizaemon |title=関ヶ原合戦史料集 |trans-title=Sekigahara Team History Collection |date=1979 |page=421|publisher=藤井治左衛門 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=WKXTAAAAMAAJ |access-date=23 May 2024 |language=Ja}}{{cite book |author1=Tadachika Kuwata |title=戦国時代の謎と怪異 |date=1977 |publisher=日本文芸社 |page=191 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=opvTAAAAMAAJ |access-date=23 May 2024 |language=Ja}}{{cite book |author1=Takashi Suzuki |title=大垣藩戶田家の見聞書 二百年間集積史料「御家耳袋」 |date=2006 |publisher=愛文書林 |isbn=4872940520 |page=32 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wBrjAAAAMAAJ |access-date=23 May 2024 |language=Ja}} After he beat the Shimazu forces, returned to Ieyasu to ask permission to participate on the Sekigahara main battle which planned in the next day. In response, Katsunari then brought his troops at midnight to surround Ogaki castle, beating a Western army led by Fukuhara Nagataka, and burning the wall and outer citadels of the castle.{{cite book |author1=岐阜県 |title=岐阜県史 Volume 6 |date=1965 |publisher=巌南堂書店 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pL9GAAAAMAAJ |access-date=21 May 2024 |language=Ja}}{{sfn|Hirai|1992}} As Katsunari burned the outer citadels of Ogaki castle,{{sfn|Hirai|1992}} the Western army nearby that area, including the Shimazu clan who had just been beaten by Katsunari's army, decided to abort their plan to go in as they thought the Ogaki castle could not be saved. On the day of the battle, Yoshihiro and his 1500 men held ground and did not fight at all. Vastly outnumbered, Yoshihiro tried to make a charge against Ieyasu himself but after his nephew [[:en:Shimazu Toyohisa|Shimazu Toyohisa]] demanded that he not kill himself over a meaningless battle, Yoshihiro instead chose to charge straight through Ieyasu's troop to make an exit to the other side. Yoshihiro had his troops make a fighting retreat called ''Sutegamari'' (捨て奸), where until a certain number of men died holding a position and repelling an attack, the main body of the army fought as well. Though Toyohisa and the bulk of the troops died, the charge and retreat were a success with a wound dealt to [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] in the process. After beating back the chase, he picked up his wife at Sumiyoshi in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]] and returned to [[:en:Satsuma Province|Satsuma Province]] by ships. The Japanese historian [[Shiramine Jun]] studied why the Shimazu clan behaved torpidly and deduced it was because Yoshihiro had become involved in the power struggling between his brother Yoshihisa and [[Ijūin Tadamune]]. It led Yoshihiro losing the support of Yoshihisa, which trapped Yoshihiro due to the lack of support from the Shimazu clan. == Death == [[File:伊集院駅.jpg|right|280px|thumb|Statue of Yoshihiro at [[:en:Ijūin Station|Ijūin Station]]]] In 1602, after recognizing why and how Yoshihiro behaved on the battlefield, Ieyasu had the Shimazu clan retain its domain and let Yoshihiro's son [[:en:Shimazu Tadatsune|Shimazu Tadatsune]] succeed him. Yoshihiro retired to [[:en:Sakurajima|Sakurajima]] and took up teaching younger generations. He died in 1619 and several of his retainers who had fought alongside him followed him by committing [[:en:Suicide|Suicide]]. == Family == It had traditionally been believed that he became the seventeenth head of the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]] after Yoshihisa, but it is currently believed that he let Yoshihisa keep his position. Yoshihiro was essential to the Shimazu clan and both Ieyasu and Hideyoshi tried to divide the clan by treating Yoshihiro well, but treating the elder brother Yoshihisa badly, which did not succeed. He was a devoted [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], and built a monument for enemy troops during the [[:en:Japanese invasions of Korea (1592–1598)|Seven-Year War]]. ==In popular culture== ''See {{section link|People of the Sengoku period in popular culture|Shimazu Yoshihiro}}'' He is a playable character in ''[[:en:Pokémon Conquest|Pokémon Conquest]]'' (Pokémon + Nobunaga's Ambition in Japan), with his partner [[:en:Pokémon|Pokémon]] being [[:en:Gurdurr|Gurdurr]] and [[:en:Conkeldurr|Conkeldurr]].{{cite web |url=http://www.pokemonconquest.com/en-us/character/yoshihiro |title=Yoshihiro + Gurdurr - Pokemon Conquest characters|work=Pokemon|access-date=2012-06-17}} He is also a playable character in ''[[:en:Total War: Shogun 2|Total War: Shogun 2]]''. He is a playable character in the ''[[:en:Samurai Warriors|Samurai Warriors]]'' series of video games. He is a playable character in the ''[[:en:Sengoku Basara|Sengoku Basara]]'' series of video games. He is the main antagonist in [[:en:Noryang: Deadly Sea|Noryang: Deadly Sea]] and is portrayed by [[:en:Baek Yoon-sik|Baek Yoon-sik]]. == Appendix == === Footnotes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Shimazu Yoshihiro : 徳永真一郎 『島津義弘』（[[:en:光文社|光文社]]光文社文庫・1992） {{ISBN|4-334-71629-6}} later （学陽書房 人物文庫 2010th year ）{{ISBN|978-4-313-75264-1}} *Shimazu Yoshihiro : 加野厚志 『島津義弘』（PHP研究所/PHP文庫 1996） {{ISBN|4-569-56965-X}} *Shimazu Hashiru : 池宮彰一郎 『島津奔る』上下巻（[[:en:新潮社|新潮社]] 2001） Vol1. {{ISBN|4-10-140816-5}}, Vol2 {{ISBN|4-10-140817-3}} *Shimazu Yoshihiro : 江宮隆之 『島津義弘』（学研M文庫、2004 ）{{ISBN|4-05-901162-2}} *Sengokuishin Shimazutoeden : 荒川佳夫 『戦国維新 島津東征伝』全3巻（学研歴史群像新書、2004〜2006） Vol.1 {{ISBN|4-05-402676-1}}, Vol.2 {{ISBN|4-05-402864-0}} Vol.3 {{ISBN|4-05-403057-2}} *[http://www.shimazu-yoshihiro.com/ Shimazu-Yoshihiro.com -About Shimazu Yoshihiro] (Japanese) {{People of the Sengoku period|state=autocollapse}} {{Authority control}}]] {{draft categories| [[Category:1535 births]] [[Category:1619 deaths]] [[Category:Daimyo]] [[Category:People from Satsuma Domain]] [[Category:Shimazu clan]] [[Category:Deified Japanese men]] [[Category:People from Kagoshima Prefecture]] }} [[en:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ca:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[de:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[es:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[fa:شیمازو یوشی‌هیرو]] [[fi:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[fr:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[id:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[it:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[ja:島津義弘]] [[ko:시마즈 요시히로]] [[mr:शिमाझु योशिहिरो]] [[nl:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[pt:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[ru:Симадзу Ёсихиро]] [[th:ชิมาซุ โยชิฮิโระ]] [[tr:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[uk:Сімадзу Йосіхіро]] [[vi:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[zh:島津義弘]] [[zh-min-nan:Simazu Yosihiro]] {{wikidata link|Q1156288}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

{{ill|Shimazu Tadatsune|WD=Q1185372}}|father={{ill|Shimazu Takahisa|WD=Q2367308}}|relatives={{ill|Shimazu Iehisa|WD=Q1133219}}; {{ill|Shimazu Toshihisa|WD=Q860025}}; {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihisa|WD=Q955727}}|native_name=島津義弘|given_name={{ill|Yoshihiro|WD=Q8056051}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1156288]] Wikidata name: Shimazu Yoshihiro [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Samurai of the Sengoku period}} {{More citations needed|date=September 2022}} {{family name hatnote|Shimazu|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox officeholder | honorific_prefix = [[Senior Third Rank]] | name = Shimazu Yoshihiro | native_name = {{nobold|島津 義弘}} | native_name_lang = ja | commands = [[:en:Iino Castle|Iino Castle]] | predecessor = [[:en:Shimazu Yoshihisa|Shimazu Yoshihisa]] | termend = 1602 | termstart = 1587 | office = Head of [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]] | mother = Sesshō | father = [[:en:Shimazu Takahisa|Shimazu Takahisa]] | nationality = [[:en:Japanese people|Japanese]] | battles = {{tree list}} *'''Early Battles''' **[[:en:Battle of Kizaki|Battle of Kizaki]] **[[:en:Siege of Takabaru|Siege of Takabaru]] **[[:en:Battle of Mimigawa|Battle of Mimigawa]] **[[:en:Siege of Iwaya Castle|Siege of Iwaya Castle]] *'''[[:en:Kyūshū Campaign|Kyūshū Campaign]]''' **[[:en:siege of Kagoshima|siege of Kagoshima]] *'''[[:en:Japanese invasions of Korea (1592–1598)|Korean campaign]]''' **'''Imjin War''' ***[[:en:Gangwon campaign|Gangwon campaign]] **'''Jeongyu War''' ***Battle of Chilchonryang ***[[:en:Battle of Sacheon (1598)|Battle of Sacheon]] ***[[:en:Battle of Noryang|Battle of Noryang]] *'''[[:en:Sekigahara Campaign|Sekigahara Campaign]]''' **Battle of Sone castle **[[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] {{tree list/end}} | unit = [[File:Maru_juji.svg|15px]] [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]] | relations = [[:en:Shimazu Yoshihisa|Shimazu Yoshihisa]] (brother)

(Oni-Shimazu) | birth_place = [[:en:Izaku Castle|Izaku Castle]], [[:en:Satsuma Province|Satsuma Province]] | death_date = August 30, 1619 (aged 84) | birth_date = August 21, 1535 | caption = Shimazu Yoshihiro | image = Shimazu Yoshihiro.jpg | Rank = [[Daimyo]] | successor = [[:en:Shimazu Tadatsune|Shimazu Tadatsune]] | spouse = Hongō Tadataka's daughter
Saishō-dono | children = Shimazu Hisayasu
[[:en:Shimazu Tadatsune|Shimazu Tadatsune]] }} {{nihongo|'''Shimazu Yoshihiro'''|島津 義弘||August 21, 1535 – August 30, 1619}} was the second son of [[:en:Shimazu Takahisa|Shimazu Takahisa]] and the younger brother of [[:en:Shimazu Yoshihisa|Shimazu Yoshihisa]]. Traditionally believed to be the 17th head of the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]], he was a skilled general during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] who greatly contributed to the unification of [[Kyushu|Kyūshū]]. == Early life and rise == He is said to have been born in [[:en:Izaku Castle|Izaku Castle]] in 1535.{{cite web|url=http://www.m-satsuma.info/detail/tableware/pg167.html|title=国史跡 伊作城|publisher=南さつま観光|language=Japanese|access-date=25 July 2019|archive-date=5 August 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190805211029/http://www.m-satsuma.info/detail/tableware/pg167.html|url-status=dead}} He was the castle lord in command of [[:en:Iino Castle|Iino Castle]].{{cite web|url=https://kojodan.jp/castle/595/ |title=国史跡 飯野城|publisher=攻城団|language=Japanese|access-date=25 July 2019}} Yoshihiro along with [[:en:Niiro Tadamoto|Niiro Tadamoto]] defeated the [[:en:Itō clan|Itō clan]] at the [[:en:Battle of Kizaki|Battle of Kizaki]], in 1572, and defeated the [[:en:Otomo clan|Otomo clan]] with his brothers in the 1578 at [[:en:Battle of Mimigawa|Battle of Mimigawa]].{{cite book |last1=Turnbull |first1=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |date=2000 |publisher=Cassell & C0 |isbn=1-85409-523-4 |location=London |page=221,272–275}} In 1587, facing [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s troops that sought to pacify [[:en:Kyūshū Campaign|Kyūshū]], Yoshihiro pressed for war even after his brother and the head of clan Yoshihisa surrendered. After Yoshihisa repeatedly asked for the surrender, Yoshihiro finally accepted. After Yoshihisa became a Buddhist monk, it was believed that he became the head of the clan, though the real power remained in Yoshihisa's hands. ==Service under Hideyoshi== He had been a willing and skillful general under Toyotomi Hideyoshi. In both 1592 and 1597 of the Seven-Year War, Yoshihiro set his foot on the Korean peninsula and successfully carried out a series of battles. In 1597, working together with [[:en:Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takatora]], [[:en:Katō Yoshiaki|Katō Yoshiaki]] and [[:en:Konishi Yukinaga|Konishi Yukinaga]], Yoshihiro defeated [[:en:Won Kyun|Won Kyun]]'s navy. At the [[:en:Battle of Sacheon (1598)|Battle of Sacheon]] (泗川) in 1598, facing a Ming army counting 37,000, Yoshihiro defeated them with only 7,000 soldiers. On the final battle of the war in 1598, the [[:en:Battle of Noryang|Battle of Noryang]], Yoshihiro's objective was to cross the Noryang Strait, link up with Konishi and retreat to Japan.The Korean admiral [[:en:Yi Sun-sin|Yi Sun-sin]] who had obstructed Yoshiaki died in this battle. Afterward, Yoshihiro rescued the Japanese commanders and returned to Japan. == Battle of Sekigahara == [[File:Shimazu Yoshihiro Large Battle Standards.jpg|left|250px|thumb|Shimazu Yoshihiro Battle Standards]] After Toyotomi Hideyoshi's death in 1598, the land became split between Toyotomi supporters backing [[Ishida Mitsunari]] and those that supported [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]], eventually culminating in the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in 1600. According to novels ([[Rakusuishū]] 『落穂集』) of the Edo Period, Yoshihiro was initially supposed to Ieyasu's side. He was crushed by [[Ishida Mitsunari]]'s army upon his arrival for a rescue [[:en:Torii Mototada|Torii Mototada]] during the siege of Fushimi Castle and, after being humiliated, he took the side of Ishida Mitsunari instead. However, recent research indicated that Yoshihiro had chosen to side with Mitsunari from the very start, before [[:en:Mōri Terumoto|Mōri Terumoto]] announced the declaration of war against the Tokugawa in July 1600. Yoshihiro also played an important role as a member of Mitsunari's core staff and was pivotal in persuading [[:en:Uesugi Kagekatsu|Uesugi Kagekatsu]] to ally with them. On September 13, Shimazu Yoshihiro led his soldiers to besiege Sone Castle and bombarding Sone castle with their artilleries. [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] and [[:en:Honda Tadakatsu|Honda Tadakatsu]] implored [[:en:Mizuno Katsushige|Mizuno Katsunari]] to repulse the Shimazu forces. In response, Katsunari went out with sallying force with his younger brother, Mizuno Tadatane, to defend Sone Castle.{{sfn|Hirai|1992}} Katsunari ordered his artilleries to return fire at the turret of the Shimazu artilleries, then he led his army to storm the Shimazu position and manage to overcome the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]]'s army, causing Yoshihiro to retreat and abandon the siege against Sone castle.{{cite book |author1=Fujii Jizaemon |title=関ヶ原合戦史料集 |trans-title=Sekigahara Team History Collection |date=1979 |page=421|publisher=藤井治左衛門 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=WKXTAAAAMAAJ |access-date=23 May 2024 |language=Ja}}{{cite book |author1=Tadachika Kuwata |title=戦国時代の謎と怪異 |date=1977 |publisher=日本文芸社 |page=191 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=opvTAAAAMAAJ |access-date=23 May 2024 |language=Ja}}{{cite book |author1=Takashi Suzuki |title=大垣藩戶田家の見聞書 二百年間集積史料「御家耳袋」 |date=2006 |publisher=愛文書林 |isbn=4872940520 |page=32 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wBrjAAAAMAAJ |access-date=23 May 2024 |language=Ja}} After he beat the Shimazu forces, returned to Ieyasu to ask permission to participate on the Sekigahara main battle which planned in the next day. However, Ieyasu instead ordered Katsunari to guard Sone castle and keep an eyes on Ogaki castle which located nearby and being controlled by Western army loyal to Mitsunari. In response, Katsunari then brought his troops at midnight to surround Ogaki castle, beating a Western army led by Fukuhara Nagataka, and burning the wall and outer citadels of the castle.{{cite book |author1=岐阜県 |title=岐阜県史 Volume 6 |date=1965 |publisher=巌南堂書店 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pL9GAAAAMAAJ |access-date=21 May 2024 |language=Ja}}{{sfn|Hirai|1992}} As Katsunari burned the outer citadels of Ogaki castle,{{sfn|Hirai|1992}} the Western army nearby that area, including the Shimazu clan who had just been beaten by Katsunari's army, decided to abort their plan to go in as they thought the Ogaki castle could not be saved. Then Yoshihiro decided to retreat into [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]].{{efn|the memorandum about Sekigahara campaign has theorized that the castle was still not fallen at that moment. However, Yoshihiro saw the smoke soared high from the Ogaki castle and though the castle already fallen.{{cite book |title=大重平六覚書 |trans-title=Memorandum of Ōshige Heiroku |url=https://dc.lib.kagoshima-u.ac.jp/en/search/display/0102700001 |access-date=21 May 2024 |language=Ja}}}} According to the scripts of Yoshihiro's subordinate [[Kando Kutarō]], Yoshihiro got along with Mitsunari from the start, but their relationship was distorted by the novelists of the Edo Period. It was said that Mitsunari did not listen to any of Yoshihiro's plans, including a surprise night attack on the day before the actual battle of Sekigahara. On the day of the battle, Yoshihiro and his 1500 men held ground and did not fight at all. After the rest of Mitsunari's side was wiped out, Yoshihiro was stranded among at least 30,000 of Ieyasu's troops. Vastly outnumbered, Yoshihiro tried to make a charge against Ieyasu himself but after his nephew [[:en:Shimazu Toyohisa|Shimazu Toyohisa]] demanded that he not kill himself over a meaningless battle, Yoshihiro instead chose to charge straight through Ieyasu's troop to make an exit to the other side. Yoshihiro had his troops make a fighting retreat called ''Sutegamari'' (捨て奸), where until a certain number of men died holding a position and repelling an attack, the main body of the army fought as well. Though Toyohisa and the bulk of the troops died, the charge and retreat were a success with a wound dealt to [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] in the process. After beating back the chase, he picked up his wife at Sumiyoshi in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]] and returned to [[:en:Satsuma Province|Satsuma Province]] by ships. The Japanese historian [[Shiramine Jun]] studied why the Shimazu clan behaved torpidly and deduced it was because Yoshihiro had become involved in the power struggling between his brother Yoshihisa and [[Ijūin Tadamune]]. It led Yoshihiro losing the support of Yoshihisa, which trapped Yoshihiro due to the lack of support from the Shimazu clan. == Death == [[File:伊集院駅.jpg|right|280px|thumb|Statue of Yoshihiro at [[:en:Ijūin Station|Ijūin Station]]]] In 1602, after recognizing why and how Yoshihiro behaved on the battlefield, Ieyasu had the Shimazu clan retain its domain and let Yoshihiro's son [[:en:Shimazu Tadatsune|Shimazu Tadatsune]] succeed him. Yoshihiro retired to [[:en:Sakurajima|Sakurajima]] and took up teaching younger generations. He died in 1619 and several of his retainers who had fought alongside him followed him by committing [[:en:Suicide|Suicide]]. == Family == It had traditionally been believed that he became the seventeenth head of the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]] after Yoshihisa, but it is currently believed that he let Yoshihisa keep his position. Yoshihiro was essential to the Shimazu clan and both Ieyasu and Hideyoshi tried to divide the clan by treating Yoshihiro well, but treating the elder brother Yoshihisa badly, which did not succeed. He was a devoted [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], and built a monument for enemy troops during the [[:en:Japanese invasions of Korea (1592–1598)|Seven-Year War]]. ==In popular culture== ''See {{section link|People of the Sengoku period in popular culture|Shimazu Yoshihiro}}'' He is a playable character in ''[[:en:Pokémon Conquest|Pokémon Conquest]]'' (Pokémon + Nobunaga's Ambition in Japan), with his partner [[:en:Pokémon|Pokémon]] being [[:en:Gurdurr|Gurdurr]] and [[:en:Conkeldurr|Conkeldurr]].{{cite web |url=http://www.pokemonconquest.com/en-us/character/yoshihiro |title=Yoshihiro + Gurdurr - Pokemon Conquest characters|work=Pokemon|access-date=2012-06-17}} He is also a playable character in ''[[:en:Total War: Shogun 2|Total War: Shogun 2]]''. He is a playable character in the ''[[:en:Samurai Warriors|Samurai Warriors]]'' series of video games. He is a playable character in the ''[[:en:Sengoku Basara|Sengoku Basara]]'' series of video games. He is the main antagonist in [[:en:Noryang: Deadly Sea|Noryang: Deadly Sea]] and is portrayed by [[:en:Baek Yoon-sik|Baek Yoon-sik]]. == Appendix == === Footnotes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Shimazu Yoshihiro : 徳永真一郎 『島津義弘』（[[:en:光文社|光文社]]光文社文庫・1992） {{ISBN|4-334-71629-6}} later （学陽書房 人物文庫 2010th year ）{{ISBN|978-4-313-75264-1}} *Shimazu Yoshihiro : 加野厚志 『島津義弘』（PHP研究所/PHP文庫 1996） {{ISBN|4-569-56965-X}} *Shimazu Hashiru : 池宮彰一郎 『島津奔る』上下巻（[[:en:新潮社|新潮社]] 2001） Vol1. {{ISBN|4-10-140816-5}}, Vol2 {{ISBN|4-10-140817-3}} *Shimazu Yoshihiro : 江宮隆之 『島津義弘』（学研M文庫、2004 ）{{ISBN|4-05-901162-2}} *Sengokuishin Shimazutoeden : 荒川佳夫 『戦国維新 島津東征伝』全3巻（学研歴史群像新書、2004〜2006） Vol.1 {{ISBN|4-05-402676-1}}, Vol.2 {{ISBN|4-05-402864-0}} Vol.3 {{ISBN|4-05-403057-2}} *[http://www.shimazu-yoshihiro.com/ Shimazu-Yoshihiro.com -About Shimazu Yoshihiro] (Japanese) {{People of the Sengoku period|state=autocollapse}} {{Authority control}}]] {{draft categories| [[Category:1535 births]] [[Category:1619 deaths]] [[Category:Daimyo]] [[Category:People from Satsuma Domain]] [[Category:Shimazu clan]] [[Category:Deified Japanese men]] [[Category:People from Kagoshima Prefecture]] }} [[en:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ca:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[de:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[es:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[fa:شیمازو یوشی‌هیرو]] [[fi:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[fr:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[id:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[it:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[ja:島津義弘]] [[ko:시마즈 요시히로]] [[mr:शिमाझु योशिहिरो]] [[nl:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[pt:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[ru:Симадзу Ёсихиро]] [[th:ชิมาซุ โยชิฮิโระ]] [[tr:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[uk:Сімадзу Йосіхіро]] [[vi:Shimazu Yoshihiro]] [[zh:島津義弘]] [[zh-min-nan:Simazu Yosihiro]] {{wikidata link|Q1156288}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[File:大一大万大吉.svg|15px]] [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Western Army]] | nickname = "Shimazu Demon"
(Oni-Shimazu) | birth_place = [[:en:Izaku Castle|Izaku Castle]], [[:en:Satsuma Province|Satsuma Province]] | death_date = August 30, 1619 (aged 84) | birth_date = August 21, 1535 | caption = Shimazu Yoshihiro | image = Shimazu Yoshihiro.jpg | Rank = [[Daimyo]] | successor = [[:en:Shimazu Tadatsune|Shimazu Tadatsune]] | spouse = Hongō Tadataka's daughter
Saishō-dono | children = Shimazu Hisayasu
[[:en:Shimazu Tadatsune|Shimazu Tadatsune]] }} {{nihongo|'''Shimazu Yoshihiro'''|島津 義弘||August 21, 1535 – August 30, 1619}} was the second son of [[:en:Shimazu Takahisa|Shimazu Takahisa]] and the younger brother of [[:en:Shimazu Yoshihisa|Shimazu Yoshihisa]]. Traditionally believed to be the 17th head of the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]], he was a skilled general during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] who greatly contributed to the unification of [[Kyushu|Kyūshū]]. == Early life and rise == He is said to have been born in [[:en:Izaku Castle|Izaku Castle]] in 1535.{{cite web|url=http://www.m-satsuma.info/detail/tableware/pg167.html|title=国史跡 伊作城|publisher=南さつま観光|language=Japanese|access-date=25 July 2019|archive-date=5 August 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190805211029/http://www.m-satsuma.info/detail/tableware/pg167.html|url-status=dead}} He was the castle lord in command of [[:en:Iino Castle|Iino Castle]].{{cite web|url=https://kojodan.jp/castle/595/ |title=国史跡 飯野城|publisher=攻城団|language=Japanese|access-date=25 July 2019}} Yoshihiro along with [[:en:Niiro Tadamoto|Niiro Tadamoto]] defeated the [[:en:Itō clan|Itō clan]] at the [[:en:Battle of Kizaki|Battle of Kizaki]], in 1572, and defeated the [[:en:Otomo clan|Otomo clan]] with his brothers in the 1578 at [[:en:Battle of Mimigawa|Battle of Mimigawa]].{{cite book |last1=Turnbull |first1=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |date=2000 |publisher=Cassell & C0 |isbn=1-85409-523-4 |location=London |page=221,272–275}} In 1587, facing [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s troops that sought to pacify [[:en:Kyūshū Campaign|Kyūshū]], Yoshihiro pressed for war even after his brother and the head of clan Yoshihisa surrendered. After Yoshihisa repeatedly asked for the surrender, Yoshihiro finally accepted. After Yoshihisa became a Buddhist monk, it was believed that he became the head of the clan, though the real power remained in Yoshihisa's hands. ==Service under Hideyoshi== He had been a willing and skillful general under Toyotomi Hideyoshi. In both 1592 and 1597 of the Seven-Year War, Yoshihiro set his foot on the Korean peninsula and successfully carried out a series of battles. In 1597, working together with [[:en:Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takatora]], [[:en:Katō Yoshiaki|Katō Yoshiaki]] and [[:en:Konishi Yukinaga|Konishi Yukinaga]], Yoshihiro defeated [[:en:Won Kyun|Won Kyun]]'s navy. At the [[:en:Battle of Sacheon (1598)|Battle of Sacheon]] (泗川) in 1598, facing a Ming army counting 37,000, Yoshihiro defeated them with only 7,000 soldiers. On the final battle of the war in 1598, the [[:en:Battle of Noryang|Battle of Noryang]], Yoshihiro's objective was to cross the Noryang Strait, link up with Konishi and retreat to Japan.The Korean admiral [[:en:Yi Sun-sin|Yi Sun-sin]] who had obstructed Yoshiaki died in this battle. Afterward, Yoshihiro rescued the Japanese commanders and returned to Japan. == Battle of Sekigahara == [[File:Shimazu Yoshihiro Large Battle Standards.jpg|left|250px|thumb|Shimazu Yoshihiro Battle Standards]] After Toyotomi Hideyoshi's death in 1598, the land became split between Toyotomi supporters backing [[Ishida Mitsunari]] and those that supported [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]], eventually culminating in the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in 1600. According to novels ([[Rakusuishū]] 『落穂集』) of the Edo Period, Yoshihiro was initially supposed to Ieyasu's side. He was crushed by [[Ishida Mitsunari]]'s army upon his arrival for a rescue [[:en:Torii Mototada|Torii Mototada]] during the siege of Fushimi Castle and, after being humiliated, he took the side of Ishida Mitsunari instead. However, recent research indicated that Yoshihiro had chosen to side with Mitsunari from the very start, before [[:en:Mōri Terumoto|Mōri Terumoto]] announced the declaration of war against the Tokugawa in July 1600. Yoshihiro also played an important role as a member of Mitsunari's core staff and was pivotal in persuading [[:en:Uesugi Kagekatsu|Uesugi Kagekatsu]] to ally with them. On September 13, Shimazu Yoshihiro led his soldiers to besiege Sone Castle and bombarding Sone castle with their artilleries. [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] and [[:en:Honda Tadakatsu|Honda Tadakatsu]] implored [[:en:Mizuno Katsushige|Mizuno Katsunari]] to repulse the Shimazu forces. In response, Katsunari went out with sallying force with his younger brother, Mizuno Tadatane, to defend Sone Castle.{{sfn|Hirai|1992}} Katsunari ordered his artilleries to return fire at the turret of the Shimazu artilleries, then he led his army to storm the Shimazu position and manage to overcome the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]]'s army, causing Yoshihiro to retreat and abandon the siege against Sone castle.{{cite book |author1=Fujii Jizaemon |title=関ヶ原合戦史料集 |trans-title=Sekigahara Team History Collection |date=1979 |page=421|publisher=藤井治左衛門 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=WKXTAAAAMAAJ |access-date=23 May 2024 |language=Ja}}{{cite book |author1=Tadachika Kuwata |title=戦国時代の謎と怪異 |date=1977 |publisher=日本文芸社 |page=191 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=opvTAAAAMAAJ |access-date=23 May 2024 |language=Ja}}{{cite book |author1=Takashi Suzuki |title=大垣藩戶田家の見聞書 二百年間集積史料「御家耳袋」 |date=2006 |publisher=愛文書林 |isbn=4872940520 |page=32 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wBrjAAAAMAAJ |access-date=23 May 2024 |language=Ja}} After he beat the Shimazu forces, returned to Ieyasu to ask permission to participate on the Sekigahara main battle which planned in the next day. However, Ieyasu instead ordered Katsunari to guard Sone castle and keep an eyes on Ogaki castle which located nearby and being controlled by Western army loyal to Mitsunari. In response, Katsunari then brought his troops at midnight to surround Ogaki castle, beating a Western army led by Fukuhara Nagataka, and burning the wall and outer citadels of the castle.{{cite book |author1=岐阜県 |title=岐阜県史 Volume 6 |date=1965 |publisher=巌南堂書店 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pL9GAAAAMAAJ |access-date=21 May 2024 |language=Ja}}{{sfn|Hirai|1992}} As Katsunari burned the outer citadels of Ogaki castle,{{sfn|Hirai|1992}} the Western army nearby that area, including the Shimazu clan who had just been beaten by Katsunari's army, decided to abort their plan to go in as they thought the Ogaki castle could not be saved. Then Yoshihiro decided to retreat into [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]].{{efn|the memorandum about Sekigahara campaign has theorized that the castle was still not fallen at that moment. However, Yoshihiro saw the smoke soared high from the Ogaki castle and though the castle already fallen.{{cite book |title=大重平六覚書 |trans-title=Memorandum of Ōshige Heiroku |url=https://dc.lib.kagoshima-u.ac.jp/en/search/display/0102700001 |access-date=21 May 2024 |language=Ja}}}} According to the scripts of Yoshihiro's subordinate [[Kando Kutarō]], Yoshihiro got along with Mitsunari from the start, but their relationship was distorted by the novelists of the Edo Period. It was said that Mitsunari did not listen to any of Yoshihiro's plans, including a surprise night attack on the day before the actual battle of Sekigahara. On the day of the battle, Yoshihiro and his 1500 men held ground and did not fight at all. After the rest of Mitsunari's side was wiped out, Yoshihiro was stranded among at least 30,000 of Ieyasu's troops. Vastly outnumbered, Yoshihiro tried to make a charge against Ieyasu himself but after his nephew [[:en:Shimazu Toyohisa|Shimazu Toyohisa]] demanded that he not kill himself over a meaningless battle, Yoshihiro instead chose to charge straight through Ieyasu's troop to make an exit to the other side. Yoshihiro had his troops make a fighting retreat called ''Sutegamari'' (捨て奸), where until a certain number of men died holding a position and repelling an attack, the main body of the army fought as well. Though Toyohisa and the bulk of the troops died, the charge and retreat were a success with a wound dealt to [[:en:Ii Naomasa|Ii Naomasa]] in the process. After beating back the chase, he picked up his wife at Sumiyoshi in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]] and returned to [[:en:Satsuma Province|Satsuma Province]] by ships. The Japanese historian [[Shiramine Jun]] studied why the Shimazu clan behaved torpidly and deduced it was because Yoshihiro had become involved in the power struggling between his brother Yoshihisa and [[Ijūin Tadamune]]. It led Yoshihiro losing the support of Yoshihisa, which trapped Yoshihiro due to the lack of support from the Shimazu clan. == Death == [[File:伊集院駅.jpg|right|280px|thumb|Statue of Yoshihiro at [[:en:Ijūin Station|Ijūin Station]]]] In 1602, after recognizing why and how Yoshihiro behaved on the battlefield, Ieyasu had the Shimazu clan retain its domain and let Yoshihiro's son [[:en:Shimazu Tadatsune|Shimazu Tadatsune]] succeed him. Yoshihiro retired to [[:en:Sakurajima|Sakurajima]] and took up teaching younger generations. He died in 1619 and several of his retainers who had fought alongside him followed him by committing [[:en:Suicide|Suicide]]. == Family == It had traditionally been believed that he became the seventeenth head of the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]] after Yoshihisa, but it is currently believed that he let Yoshihisa keep his position. Yoshihiro was essential to the Shimazu clan and both Ieyasu and Hideyoshi tried to divide the clan by treating Yoshihiro well, but treating the elder brother Yoshihisa badly, which did not succeed. He was a devoted [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], and built a monument for enemy troops during the [[:en:Japanese invasions of Korea (1592–1598)|Seven-Year War]]. ==In popular culture== ''See {{section link|People of the Sengoku period in popular culture|Shimazu Yoshihiro}}'' He is a playable character in ''[[:en:Pokémon Conquest|Pokémon Conquest]]'' (Pokémon + Nobunaga's Ambition in Japan), with his partner [[:en:Pokémon|Pokémon]] being [[:en:Gurdurr|Gurdurr]] and [[:en:Conkeldurr|Conkeldurr]].{{cite web |url=http://www.pokemonconquest.com/en-us/character/yoshihiro |title=Yoshihiro + Gurdurr - Pokemon Conquest characters|work=Pokemon|access-date=2012-06-17}} He is also a playable character in ''[[:en:Total War: Shogun 2|Total War: Shogun 2]]''. He is a playable character in the ''[[:en:Samurai Warriors|Samurai Warriors]]'' series of video games. He is a playable character in the ''[[:en:Sengoku Basara|Sengoku Basara]]'' series of video games. He is the main antagonist in [[:en:Noryang: Deadly Sea|Noryang: Deadly Sea]] and is portrayed by [[:en:Baek Yoon-sik|Baek Yoon-sik]]. == Appendix == === Footnotes === {{Notelist}} === References ===
